question: What is an MPIN
answer: An MPIN, or Mobile Banking Personal Identification Number, is a 4-digit passcode created during registration to secure your banking app. It ensures convenient logins and authenticates transactions, reducing fraud risks. Sharing your MPIN could lead to unauthorized access and financial losses, so keep it confidential at all times.

question: How can I create a wallet?
answer: Complete the following steps to create wallet: 1. Go to Bajaj Pay on home page 2. Tap on 'Wallet' 3. Click on 'Setup Wallet' 4. question: What happens if I enter the incorrect MPIN?
answer: You have 3 attempts to enter the correct MPIN. After 3 failed attempts, your device will be locked for 30 seconds. Once the device is locked, you can retry entering your MPIN after the 30-second period. If you have forgotten your  MPIN, you can click on the ‘Forgot MPIN’ option on your app screen and follow the instructions to reset it.

question: Why is setting MPIN important?
answer: To improve security, MPIN provides an additional layer of protection beyond just using a password, reducing the risk of unauthorized access. Anyone with the Bajaj Finserv App can set MPIN as their 2FA method to strengthen their security against cyber threats and unauthorized access. It is mandatory to set an MPIN after successfully signing up with SIM card authentication for new installations and updates.

question: What is 2FA (Two-Factor Authentication)?
answer: 2FA stands for Two-Factor Authentication. It is a security process that requires two methods of verification to access an account.

question: How is the app MPIN different from the UPI PIN?
answer: To set up your MPIN , please follow these steps: 1. Open Bajaj Finserv App 2.After Signup, Enter your 4-digit desired MPIN (choose a strong combination) 3. Re-enter your 4-digit desired MPIN 4. Choose a security question for future verification 5. Answer the security question 6. Click on 'Confirm'

question: When will I need to use the MPIN?
answer: You will be required to use the MPIN in the following circumstances: - Once activated, you will be prompted to enter your MPIN on every signup on Bajaj Finserv App. - If you don't have biometrics enabled for app login, you will be prompted to enter MPIN on every login

question: What should I keep in mind while setting MPIN, and are there any criteria?
answer: While setting up your 4-digit MPIN, you can consider choosing a code that is easier for you to remember, but difficult for anyone else to guess. Please ensure that your MPIN is not shared with anyone (not even a Bajaj Finance Limited employee), and do not write it down anywhere. It is also a good practice to keep changing your MPIN from time to time, to keep fraudsters at bay.

question: What happens if I enter the incorrect MPIN?
answer: You have 3 attempts to enter the correct MPIN. After 3 failed attempts, your device will be locked for 30 seconds. Once the device is locked, you can retry entering your MPIN after the 30-second period. If you have forgotten your  MPIN, you can click on the ‘Forgot MPIN’ option on your app screen and follow the instructions to reset it.

question: What happens if I forget my MPIN?
answer: If you forget your MPIN, you can always reset it. Please follow these steps to reset your MPIN: 1. Click on 'Forgot MPIN?' 2. Answer the security question (you will get only 3 attempts to answer) 3. Enter and re-enter your 4-digit desired MPIN (choose a strong combination) 4. Click on 'Confirm' In case you answer the security question incorrectly then, please follow this process: 1. Click on 'Try Another Method' 2. Choose 'PAN' as your mode of authentication 3. Enter your PAN details 4. Click on 'Confirm' and repeat the steps shared above to reset MPIN

question: How can I change my security question?
answer: To change your security question, please follow these steps: 1. Open App and Go to the 'Settings' option from 'Menu' 2. Click on 'Security Settings' 3. Click on 'Reset Security Question' 4. Enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number 5. Choose a security question 6. Answer the security question and click on 'Confirm'

question: What happens if I forget my MPIN? (If Already logged in with Biometric)

question: How do I install Bajaj Pay FASTag?
answer:

4. Keep the Instructions set attached to the FASTag Welcome kit handy for quick reference.

question: What is the FASTag security deposit?

question: What is Wallet Auto-Load?
answer: Wallet Auto-Load is a convenient feature that automatically adds money to your wallet when your wallet balance falls below a certain threshold. It ensures you always have enough funds to make payments or do transactions without the need for manually adding funds to wallet.

question: How does Wallet Auto-Load work?
answer: Wallet-Auto Load works by linking your Bajaj Pay UPI to your Bank account. When your balance falls below a certain threshold, it will automatically initiate a transaction to replenish your wallet with a specified amount chosen by you at the time of Auto-Load registration.

question: Why should I set up Wallet Auto-Load?
answer: Wallet Auto-Load eliminates the need of manually adding funds to your wallet and always ensures sufficient funds to make payments or do transactions without any interruption.

question: What happens if my wallet balance exceeds a certain limit with Auto-Load?

question: When will my wallet be automatically loaded?

question: What is a Collect request?
answer: A collect request is a payment request sent by a merchant or service provider to a customer for payment of goods or services. It can also be initiated by some other person.

question: Can I cancel a collect request?
answer: Yes, the collect request can be denied by the user.

question: Is there a limit on the amount that can be collected using collect request?
answer: For person to person there is limit of Rs. 2,000 and for merchant to person transactions, it can be 1 Lacs and for few specific merchants limit can be upto 2 Lacs.

question: Where can I see received collect request?

question: Is there a fee for using collect request?
answer: No, there is no fee for using the collect request feature.

question: How can I modify my added Beneficiaries?

question: How to Send Money via Wallet UPI ID?
answer:
To send money via Wallet UPI ID, you have multiple options. Click on "Pay" in the app, and a scanner page will open. You can choose one of the following options:

1. Scan and Pay: Scan any user's or merchant's UPI QR code and make the payment

2. UPI VPA: Enter any user's or merchant's UPI VPA and make the payment

3. Collect Request Approval: Receive a collect request from another UPI user or merchant and approve the payment

question: How do I Pay to Merchant through Wallet UPI ID / VPA?
answer:
To pay a merchant through Wallet UPI ID/VPA, you have multiple options. Click on "Pay" in the app, and a scanner page will open. You can choose one of the following options:

1. Scan and Pay: Scan any merchant's UPI QR code and make the payment

2. UPI VPA: Enter any merchant's UPI VPA and make the payment

3. Collect Request Approval: Receive a collect request from the merchant or merchant app and approve the payment.

4. Intent: Go to another app, and on that app's checkout page, under the UPI section, you will see Bajaj Finserv's app logo. Clicking on it will take you to the Bajaj Finserv app, where you can initiate the payment

question: What if I have sent money to the wrong UPI ID(VPA)?
answer: I regret to inform you that it is beyond the control of Bajaj Finance Limited to reverse a successfully executed transaction. Hence, please contact the recipient or beneficiary's bank for a refund.

question: How to Send Money to contacts?
answer:
To send or receive money to your contacts, both sender and receiver should have a Bajaj Pay Wallet which is KYC verified. Follow below steps to send money:

2. Enter the beneficiaries mobile number or tap on 'Contact Book' icon to access your contacts

3. Type the beneficiary name in search field or navigate and select your contact

4. Enter the amount and tap on 'Pay'

question: Can I use the same QR code for all my Wallet UPI-linked bank accounts?
answer: Yes, the QR code generated in your Wallet UPI-enabled app is typically linked to your primary UPI ID or virtual payment address (VPA). Regardless of how many bank accounts you have linked to your Wallet UPI app, the QR code remains the same for all transactions.

question: How do I update my KYC
answer: Follow the below given steps to update the KYC so you get increase in limit to transact through wallet:1. Click on mentioned link 2. Click on 'Setup Wallet' 3. Proceed to fill/confirm the self declaration details to create a Min KYC wallet 4. Tap on 'Go to Bajaj Pay Wallet' 5. Tap on 'Send to Bank' or 'Pay' or 'Upgrade Wallet KYC' banner 6. Fill in the self declaration form 7. Complete your KYC using DigiLocker 8. Click on 'Proceed to Video KYC' to start the Video Verification (Video KYC) process 9. question: How do I know if my KYC is complete?
answer: Upon successful upgrade of Wallet to Full-KYC, you can check the status: 1. Log in to the app 2. Tap on the Wallet icon on the home screen 3. Look for the settings icon on the right side and tap on it 4. If your KYC has been completed, you will see the status as 'completed'.

question: Is KYC mandatory for all users?
answer: Completing KYC is optional, but I recommend you complete your KYC in order to enjoy exciting benefits of Bajaj Pay Full KYC Wallet.

question: How can I set my own limits on Wallet?
answer:
You can set your own limits basis your KYC type within the transactional limits set by Bajaj Pay.

Currently, only monthly limits can be set on Count (no. of transactions) and Value (Rs.) for below transaction types:

Full KYC Wallet:
1. Online Payment: Bill Payment & Recharges
2. Offline Payment: Towards Bajaj Pay QR & POS devices
3. Bank Transfer
4. Transfer to other Bajaj Pay Wallet
5. Wallet UPI
 
Min KYC Wallet:
1. Online Payment: Bill Payment & Recharges
2. Offline Payment: Towards Bajaj Pay QR & POS devices

In case of Min KYC wallet, I recommend you to complete your KYC in order to enjoy exciting benefits of Bajaj Pay Full KYC Wallet.

question: What if I have sent money to the wrong UPI ID(VPA) ?
answer: I regret to inform you that it is beyond our control to reverse a successfully executed transaction. Hence, please contact the recipient or beneficiary's bank for a refund.

question: What if my payment is successful but the money has not reached the receiver ?
answer:
Please be assured that your money is safe with us. If your amount is not credited to the beneficiary's account, it will be automatically reversed to your Bajaj Pay wallet. 

In case the amount is not reversed or there is a delay, please raise a request with us by following these steps:
-Go to the 'Main Menu (Three Lines)' at the top left of the Home Screen
- Select 'Help and Support'
- Click on 'Raise a Request' at the bottom of the screen.

question: Can I cancel a successful payment I have made to another wallet ?
answer: I regret to inform you that it is beyond our control to cancel a successfully executed transaction. Hence, please contact the Recipient or Beneficiary further

question: Can I cancel a successful payment I have made to a phone number or UPI ID(VPA) ?
answer: I regret to inform you that it is beyond our control to cancel a successfully executed transaction. Hence, please contact the recipient or beneficiary for further assistance.

question: What if I have paid twice for an merchant/Bill payments ?
answer: In such cases, the merchant doesn't allow us to cancel the transaction. Please contact the customer support team of the biller directly with your transaction details.

question: What if the order is not confirmed or bill not reflecting as paid for a successful transaction ?
answer: If you do not receive confirmation of your order or booking from the merchant, I request you to reach out to them directly to inquire about the status of your request.

question: Why is my Wallet- UPI payment pending?
answer: Banks usually complete Wallet- UPI payments instantly. In rare cases, due to technical issues, banks may take additional time to deposit the payment money into the receiver's account. It is recommended that you wait for 48 hours from the time of payment to know the final status.

question: How do I cancel my pending payment ?
answer: I regret to inform you that you may not be able to cancel any pending transaction. Please note that the status may change to success or fail. For failed transactions, a refund will be initiated.

question: What if my payment is pending for more than 48 hours ?
answer: If your payment is pending for more than 48 hours. I request you to raise a query with us. question: My transaction failed, why is my money deducted for merchant transaction?
answer:
I regret for the inconvenience. If your transaction has failed but the money was deducted from your account, the bank will automatically reverse the amount to your account. It may take T+1 (T=Transaction Date) for this amount to reflect in your account, if you have made wallet transaction.
However, It may take T+5 (T=Transaction Date) for this amount to reflect in your account, if you have made wallet as an UPI transaction

question: Why did my Wallet payment fail?
answer: There could be various reasons why your wallet payment failed. Some possible scenarios include payment gateway issues, network connectivity problems, payment limits, bank server issues, or issues at the biller's end.

question: When will I receive a refund for a failed payment ?

answer: If you have exceeded your transaction limit, you may not be able to add money to your Bajaj Pay wallet. To know your Bajaj Pay wallet limits, please refer to the 'Limit Applicable' related FAQs provided on this screen.

question: Why am I unable to receive money from another Bajaj Pay wallet user ?
answer: Currently, both sender and receiver should have a Bajaj Pay Wallet which is KYC verified to transfer or receive money

question: How can I block my Bajaj Pay wallet account?
answer:

question: How can I unblock my Bajaj Pay wallet account?
answer:
To unblock your account, please follow these steps:

question: Are the documents that I have provided safe and secured?
answer: The documents provided by you are Safe and Securely stored by Bajaj Finance Limited. The company adheres to the RBI Guidelines and has robust checks and mechanisms to protect your data.

question: Where can I find the Bajaj Pay wallet payment option on other websites and apps?
answer: The option to use Bajaj Pay wallet for payments will be available at the payment checkout page.

question: How can I delink my Bajaj Pay wallet from third-party merchants?
answer: This feature is not currently available on our app. You may go to the merchant's app and delink your wallet from the checkout page.

question: If I link my Bajaj Pay wallet to a third-party merchant, what kind of access do they have to my wallet?
answer: The merchant can deduct the money equal to the transaction/order amount only. If in case, the additional amount has been debited, you should contact the merchant to refund the amount.

question: Is my money safe in the Bajaj Pay wallet if I link it to third-party merchant apps/websites?
answer: Rest assured that your money is safe with us. I are an RBI-approved Wallet service provider, and your money is secured.

question: What should I do if a merchant deducts more than agreed amount from my wallet?
answer: The merchant can deduct the money equal to the transaction/order amount only. If in case, the additional amount has been debited, you should contact the merchant to refund the amount.

question: Where can I check my previous payments?
answer:
To check all previous payments with the merchant, including amount, date, and order details, kindly go to the 'Passbook' section of your Bajaj Finserv App. Steps:
1. Log in to the Bajaj Finserv App
2. Click on 'Passbook' under 'Bajaj Pay' at the top of the Home Screen
3. Click/Scroll to the 'Bajaj Pay Wallet' section.

question: How can I transfer the Bajaj Pay wallet balance to my bank account?
answer:
Full KYC wallet users can transfer their Bajaj Pay wallet balance to registered beneficiary bank accounts by using the 'Send to Bank' option under 'My Bajaj Pay Wallet' section. 

To register a beneficiary, please follow these steps:

1. Click on the ‘Wallet’ icon under the ‘Bajaj Pay’ section
2. Click on the ‘Send to Bank’ under 'My Bajaj Pay Wallet' section
3. Click on ‘Add account’
4. Enter beneficiary bank details and click on 'Proceed'.

Please Note: Transfers can be done post 10 minutes in case of a newly registered beneficiary.

Minimum KYC Wallet users will not have an option of 'Send to Bank' and would be required to upgrade to Full KYC.

question: Do I need to pay any fee for transferring money from my Bajaj Pay wallet to any bank account?
answer: Yes, you need to pay a fee of 1% of the amount being transferred while transferring money from your Bajaj Pay wallet to any bank account.

question: What is Two-Factor Authentication?
answer:
Two-Factor Authentication is a feature that adds an extra layer of security to all transactions involving debit from your Bajaj Pay wallet. This feature is in line with RBI's Master Direction on Prepaid Payment Instruments. Every transaction involving debit from your Bajaj Pay wallet can be validated through either Device PIN, Touch ID (Fingerprints), Face ID, App M-PIN, or OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Please upgrade to the latest version to enable this functionality.

question: How can I set up the Two Factor Authentication for my Bajaj Pay wallet?
answer:
To setup up Two Factor Authentication for your Bajaj Pay wallet, please follow the below steps:
1. Go to the Main Menu (Three Lines) at the top left of the Home Screen
2. Select ‘Settings'
3. Select ‘Security Settings’
4. Enable biometrics i.e., set your Touch ID/Face ID by turning the toggle button 'Active (Green)'.

Please Note: 
In case you do not set up your Two Factor Authentication, you need to validate your transactions through the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.

question: Can I make transactions without accepting Two Factor Authentication?
answer: No, Two-Factor Authentication is required to ensure the safety and security of your Bajaj Pay wallet transactions. This helps prevent any loss that may occur due to unauthorized, suspicious, or fraudulent activity. Please note that Two-Factor Authentication is mandatory as per RBI's Master Direction on Prepaid Payment Instruments.

question: What all modes of authentication are accepted for making transactions?
answer:
To make transactions, you can use any of the below-given mode of authentication:
1. Device PIN
2. Touch ID (Fingerprints)
3. Face ID
4. M-PIN or
5. OTP sent to your registered mobile number. Please Note:
In case you do not set up your Two Factor Authentication, you need to validate your transactions through the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.

question: How many attempts are allowed for authenticating a debit transaction from Bajaj Pay Wallet?
answer: You will have a maximum of 3 attempts to complete your transaction successfully. After 3 attempts, your transaction will fail.

question: What is Wallet interoperability?
answer: Wallet Interoperability is a technical compatibility that allows you to use Bajaj Pay Wallet for making payments through Unified Payment Interface (UPI) either by using the Wallet Virtual Payment Address (Wallet VPA) or by scanning any UPI QR code.

question: What are the Features of Wallet Interoperability?
Who are eligible for Wallet Interoperability?
answer: All users having valid and active Full KYC Bajaj Pay Wallet shall be eligible for wallet interoperability, subject to other Terms and Conditions.

question: How to create Wallet UPI ID?
answer:
To create your wallet UPI ID, please follow these steps:

1. Click on 'Wallet' under 'Bajaj Pay'

2. Click on 'Activate Now'

3. Select the 'SIM Card' linked to your Bajaj Pay wallet and click on 'Proceed'

4. Your wallet UPI ID will be generated within a few seconds

Alternatively, you can also:

1. Click on the 'Settings' on the Wallet Homescreen

2. Click on ‘Link Bajaj Pay Wallet with UPI’

3. Select the 'SIM Card' linked to your Bajaj Pay wallet and click on 'Proceed'

4. Your wallet UPI ID will be generated within a few seconds

question: What is UPI ID for Wallet?
answer: Wallet UPI ID / Wallet VPA shall mean the Virtual Payment Address associated with Bajaj Pay Wallet for enabling PPI Interoperability through UPI

question: Can I choose the UPI ID for Wallet?
answer: No, the Bajaj Pay Wallet UPI Id is created by default. Currently, there is no option to choose the Bajaj Pay Wallet UPI Id.

question: How to authorize an interoperable transaction?
answer: The interoperable transaction is required to be authorized using a preferred Second Factor Authorization (Fingerprint, MPIN, and OTP etc.) method.

question: What is Second Factor Authorization?
answer: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) or Second Factor Authorization is a security system that requires two separate, distinct forms of identification in order to make payments through Bajaj Pay Wallet.

question: How do I Pay to Merchant through Wallet UPI ID / VPA?
answer: You can make payments to merchants in the following manner: - Scan any UPI QR code available at merchant outlets or on merchant websites, select UPI as the payment option and enter your wallet UPI ID to generate a collect request on your Bajaj Pay wallet. - Pay entering Customer Preferred Second Factor Authorization

question: Do I need to have a bank account to create a Wallet UPI ID ?
answer: No, you don’t need a bank account to create a Wallet UPI ID.

question: Can I create a Wallet UPI ID / VPA by using a mobile number different from number linked to my Wallet?
answer: No. Your wallet UPI ID gets created using your mobile number registered with your Wallet, by default.

question: Why am I not getting an option to create a UPI ID for wallet?
answer: Interoperability of wallet (UPI ID for Wallet) is provided only to customers having valid and active Full KYC Bajaj Pay Wallet. Please upgrade your Bajaj Pay Wallet to Full-KYC for creating Wallet UPI ID.

question: What will happen to my UPI ID for Wallet if my wallet is downgraded to Small Wallet?

question: I want to know status of the autopay
answer: If you want to know auto pay deduction status, request you to directly contact bank. For auto pay active status, request you to follow mentioned steps: i)Go to the Home Page and click on UPI.ii)Open Settings.iii)Click on Autopay Settings.iv)Navigate to the Active page.
